ddls:
CREATE TABLE "jmr_versions" (
  "jmr_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each JMRI version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of JMRI */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the JMRI version */,
  "documentation" TEXT /* File path to the documentation for the JMRI version */,
  "eol_date" TEXT /* End-of-Life date of the JMRI version */,
  "security_patches" INTEGER /* Number of security patches applied to the JMRI version */,
  "download_url" TEXT /* URL to download the JMRI version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("jmr_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "java_versions" (
  "java_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Java version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of Java */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the Java version */,
  "documentation" TEXT /* File path to the documentation for the Java version */,
  "eol_date" TEXT /* End-of-Life date of the Java version */,
  "security_patches" INTEGER /* Number of security patches applied to the Java version */,
  "download_url" TEXT /* URL to download the Java version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("java_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mac_os_versions" (
  "mac_os_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each macOS version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of macOS */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the macOS version */,
  "documentation" TEXT /* File path to the documentation for the macOS version */,
  "eol_date" TEXT /* End-of-Life date of the macOS version */,
  "security_patches" INTEGER /* Number of security patches applied to the macOS version */,
  "download_url" TEXT /* URL to download the macOS version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mac_os_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "windows_versions" (
  "windows_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Windows version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of Windows */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the Windows version */,
  "documentation" TEXT /* File path to the documentation for the Windows version */,
  "eol_date" TEXT /* End-of-Life date of the Windows version */,
  "security_patches" INTEGER /* Number of security patches applied to the Windows version */,
  "download_url" TEXT /* URL to download the Windows version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("windows_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "linux_versions" (
  "linux_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Linux version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of Linux */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the Linux version */,
  "documentation" TEXT /* File path to the documentation for the Linux version */,
  "eol_date" TEXT /* End-of-Life date of the Linux version */,
  "security_patches" INTEGER /* Number of security patches applied to the Linux version */,
  "download_url" TEXT /* URL to download the Linux version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("linux_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ubuntu_versions" (
  "ubuntu_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Ubuntu version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of Ubuntu */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the Ubuntu version */,
  "documentation" TEXT /* File path to the documentation for the Ubuntu version */,
  "eol_date" TEXT /* End-of-Life date of the Ubuntu version */,
  "security_patches" INTEGER /* Number of security patches applied to the Ubuntu version */,
  "download_url" TEXT /* URL to download the Ubuntu version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ubuntu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "raspbian_versions" (
  "raspbian_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Raspbian version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of Raspbian */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the Raspbian version */,
  "documentation" TEXT /* File path to the documentation for the Raspbian version */,
  "eol_date" TEXT /* End-of-Life date of the Raspbian version */,
  "security_patches" INTEGER /* Number of security patches applied to the Raspbian version */,
  "download_url" TEXT /* URL to download the Raspbian version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("raspbian_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compatibility_mappings" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compatibility mapping */,
  "jmr_id" INTEGER /* ID of the JMRI version */,
  "java_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Java version */,
  "mac_os_id" INTEGER /* ID of the macOS version */,
  "windows_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Windows version */,
  "linux_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Linux version */,
  "ubuntu_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Ubuntu version */,
  "raspbian_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Raspbian version */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the compatibility (e.g., tested, untested, incompatible) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the compatibility */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_mappings_linux_id FOREIGN KEY ("linux_id") REFERENCES linux_versions ("linux_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_mappings_jmr_id FOREIGN KEY ("jmr_id") REFERENCES jmr_versions ("jmr_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_mappings_java_id FOREIGN KEY ("java_id") REFERENCES java_versions ("java_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_mappings_raspbian_id FOREIGN KEY ("raspbian_id") REFERENCES raspbian_versions ("raspbian_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_mappings_mac_os_id FOREIGN KEY ("mac_os_id") REFERENCES mac_os_versions ("mac_os_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_mappings_ubuntu_id FOREIGN KEY ("ubuntu_id") REFERENCES ubuntu_versions ("ubuntu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_mappings_windows_id FOREIGN KEY ("windows_id") REFERENCES windows_versions ("windows_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "release_dates" (
  "release_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each release date */,
  "jmr_id" INTEGER /* ID of the JMRI version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the JMRI version */,
  "eol_date" TEXT /* End-of-Life date of the JMRI version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("release_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_release_dates_jmr_id FOREIGN KEY ("jmr_id") REFERENCES jmr_versions ("jmr_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documentations" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each documentation */,
  "jmr_id" INTEGER /* ID of the JMRI version */,
  "documentation" TEXT /* File path to the documentation for the JMRI version */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the documentation */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update date of the documentation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_documentations_jmr_id FOREIGN KEY ("jmr_id") REFERENCES jmr_versions ("jmr_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "active_status" TEXT /* Active status of the user account (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the information */,
  "jmr_id" INTEGER /* ID of the JMRI version being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the information was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the information */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user accessing the information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_jmr_id FOREIGN KEY ("jmr_id") REFERENCES jmr_versions ("jmr_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
